problem_spec.summary: Evaluate a Boolean formula on all satisfying valuations under an additional parity or threshold filter, encode each surviving valuation as an integer, and aggregate the encoded values by an explicit arithmetic expression modulo a fixed modulus.
ask: Compute the stated modular aggregate over the surviving valuations.

parameter(s).name: variable_count
parameter(s).purpose: sets the size of the truth table to be enumerated

invariant(s).name: binary_encoding_is_unique
invariant(s).statement: Each admissible Boolean valuation maps to a unique integer under the stated binary-weight encoding.

invariant(s).name: finite_search_space
invariant(s).statement: The problem reduces to evaluating finitely many Boolean assignments, namely 2^(variable_count) candidate valuations.

essential_assumption(s): Boolean connectives are interpreted by the usual truth-functional semantics.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Exhaustive evaluation of the finite truth table determines a single aggregate value before the final modular reduction.

technique(s).name: exhaustive_truth_table_filtering
technique(s).purpose: identify exactly which valuations contribute to the aggregate

technique(s).name: weighted_integer_aggregation
technique(s).purpose: compute the requested arithmetic sum and reduce it modulo the stated modulus

theorem(s).name: propositional_semantics
theorem(s).role: gives the exact truth value of each Boolean formula under each assignment

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: A finite Boolean search space can be checked exactly by exhaustive enumeration.

critical_trick(s): Convert each surviving assignment to its encoded integer and aggregate only after the logical filter has been applied.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: iterate over all Boolean valuations exactly

task(s).name: truth_table_verification
task(s).reason: recompute the final residue by exhaustive enumeration
